---
title: "Catherine Anna Mona Brougham"
---

## Catherine Anna Mona Brougham

**Dates:** (1804/5-1892)

**Maiden name:** (Macartney)

### Description

contributor to <i>MP</i> (1888)

### Biography

MS Girton College, Cambridge, To A.D. Innes (9 June 1888), identifies the contributor of an article signed \'C.A.W.B.\' as \'Mrs Brougham/ Castlehaven Rectory/ Castletownsend\'.  The article is signed ‘C.A.W.B.’, and refers to ‘my mother, Lady M.’ and to the author as ‘aged’. The initials were evidently misprinted, and the author Catherine Anna Mona Brougham (1804/5-1892), widow of the Rev. Henry Brougham (d.1831), Rector of Tallow, and daughter of Sir John Macartney and his second wife Catherine Burgh.    Two other articles appeared in the <i>Monthly Packet</i> in 1889 over the initials \'C.A.M.B.\'  Mrs Brougham was probably living with a clerical son, because E. Œ. Somerville, who lived in Castletownsend, refers (10 September 1888) to \'a hideous tennis function at the Broughams.\'  <i>Selected Letters of Somerville and Ross</i> ed. G. Lewis (London: Faber 1989), 118. Earlier \'Recollections of Parochial Work in Ireland\' had appeared in <i>MP</i> 1864-5, and the bank account shows payments to Mrs Brougham  of £11 (20 April 1863); £10 (12 March 1864); £5 15 <i>s</i>. (29 August 1864); £8 (31 Jan 1865); £3.3s. (23 February 1866). We are grateful to Mr Peter Wyly for supplying her dates of birth and death. [See also PB Brougham]

### Letters

- Letter from June 9th [1888]

---

[← Back to all letters](../letters.html)
